name: MCPDocSearch — Documentation Search MCP
what_it_does: MCPDocSearch MCP server enabling AI agents to search documentation sites — crawling and indexing documentation from any URL, performing semantic search over indexed docs, and integrating documentation search into agent-driven coding, research, and knowledge management workflows.
use_cases: Searching documentation for libraries and frameworks from coding agents
Indexing custom internal documentation from knowledge base agents
Finding API references and guides from assistant agents
Building searchable doc indexes for RAG pipelines from knowledge agents
Answering questions from specific documentation sites from specialized agents
Keeping agents updated with latest library docs from DevEx agents
not_for: General web search (use Serper or Brave for broader web search)
Teams using Dash or Zeal for offline documentation (use Dash MCP)
Real-time documentation that changes faster than crawl frequency
best_when: An agent needs to search specific documentation sites — library docs, API references, or custom internal documentation that isn't in the model's training data.
avoid_when: You need general web search or have documentation available in Dash/Zeal format — those tools have better offline doc coverage.

auth.notes: No authentication required for public documentation sites. Authentication not supported for private/internal docs requiring login.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — open source local MCP server
pricing.notes: Free and open source. Crawls public docs locally. No external API costs.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by network and indexed doc size; no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Documentation must be crawled and indexed before search is available — not instant
Crawling large documentation sites may take time and significant disk space
JavaScript-rendered documentation may not crawl correctly — static sites work best
Search quality depends on crawl completeness — partial crawls give incomplete results
Community MCP from individual contributor — maintenance not guaranteed
Index must be refreshed manually when documentation is updated
agent_readiness.security_notes: Crawls public URLs locally. No external credentials. Community MCP. No secrets needed. Index stored locally — be aware of disk space for large documentation sets.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public URL crawling. No secrets. Community MCP. Local index storage.
